featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering

    Create visualization comparison of original vs filtered feature tables

    plot_qc_comparison.Rd

    Generates a comprehensive set of plots to compare original and filtered tables. Includes coverage distributions, sparsity histograms, retention barplots, top features barplots, and heatmaps.

    Plots generated: sample coverage distribution (log10 scale, faceted), feature abundance distribution (log10 scale, faceted), sample sparsity histogram (per-sample sparsity distribution, faceted with mean lines), retention rates barplot (read, feature, sample retention), top N features stacked barplot (relative abundance comparison), heatmap of top variable features (original and filtered separately).

    plot_qc_comparison(original_table, filtered_table,
                    plot_dir = NULL, prefix = "qc_comparison",
                    top_n = 10, heatmap_top = 50,
                    width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

    Arguments

    original_table

    Original feature table (data.frame) before filtering.

    filtered_table

    Filtered feature table (data.frame) after filtering.

    plot_dir

    Optional directory path to save plots. If NULL, plots are displayed only.

    prefix

    Prefix for saved plot filenames. Default is "qc_comparison".

    top_n

    Number of top features to show in barplots. Default is 10.

    heatmap_top

    Number of top variable features to show in heatmap. Default is 50.

    width

    Plot width in inches for ggsave. Default 10.

    height

    Plot height in inches for ggsave. Default 8.

    dpi

    DPI for saved raster output. Default 300.

    Value

    A list containing:

    plots

    Named list of ggplot objects

    paths

    Named list of file paths (if plot_dir provided)

    See also

    compute_filtering_qc for numerical QC metrics. plot_presence_analysis for presence frequency analysis.

    Examples

    # Display plots
# plot_qc_comparison(original_table, filtered_table)

# Save plots to directory
# plot_qc_comparison(original_table, filtered_table, plot_dir = "qc_plots", prefix = "my_analysis")

    Contents

    Developed by Vojtech Barton.
